metadata.website_type: developer documentation for ai evaluation platform

tasks.name: Create a Python quickstart evaluation with accuracy and F1 on 200 samples
tasks.description: Using the developer docs, locate the Python quickstart for text classification, configure an evaluation that uses both accuracy and F1 metrics, sets max_samples to 200, uses model id "gpt-4.1-mini", and copy the final code snippet.
steps: Navigate to the documentation homepage of the AI evaluation platform.
In the left sidebar, click the "Getting started" section.
Under "Getting started", click the link titled "Quickstart: Text classification".
On the quickstart page, locate the language tabs above the first code sample and click the "Python" tab.
Scroll down to the section labeled "Run your first evaluation" and locate the main Python code block that defines the evaluation configuration.
Click inside the code block where the `metrics` list or array is defined and edit it so it contains exactly the strings "accuracy" and "f1".
In the same code block, locate the configuration field for `max_samples` (or similarly named field) and set its value to `200`.
Still in the same code block, locate the `model` (or `model_id`) parameter and change its value to the string "gpt-4.1-mini".
Click the "Copy" button in the top-right corner of this Python code block to copy the configured evaluation script.

tasks.name: Build a curl command for the batch evaluation REST endpoint with limit 50 and cursor "cursor_abc"
tasks.description: Use the API reference in the docs to find the REST endpoint for creating a batch evaluation run, then construct a curl example that sets project_id to "proj_123" and includes query parameters limit=50 and cursor="cursor_abc", and copy the final curl command.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click the "API Reference" item in the top navigation bar.
In the API sidebar, expand the section labeled "Evaluations" if it is collapsed.
Within the "Evaluations" section, click the endpoint description that includes the phrase "Create batch evaluation" or "Batch runs".
On the endpoint page, locate the language tabs above the example requests and click the "curl" tab.
In the displayed curl example, click inside the URL and replace the placeholder project identifier in the path (e.g., `{project_id}`) with the literal string `proj_123`.
Append the query string `?limit=50&cursor=cursor_abc` to the end of the request URL inside the curl command, ensuring it appears after the path.
If the example includes a JSON body, leave it unchanged and scroll as needed until the entire curl command is visible.
Click the "Copy" icon or button associated with this curl code block to copy the full batch evaluation request with the updated project_id and query parameters.

tasks.name: Select the cost metric for token-based billing and copy its JSON configuration example
tasks.description: From the metrics documentation, choose the metric whose description mentions token-based billing (the cost metric), update its JSON configuration example so price_per_1k_tokens is 0.002 with currency "USD" and unit "tokens", and copy the JSON.
steps: Navigate to the documentation homepage.
In the top navigation bar, click the "Concepts" section.
Within the Concepts sidebar, click the entry labeled "Metrics" or "Metrics overview".
On the metrics overview page, locate the list or grid of available metrics and click the metric whose short description mentions "token-based billing" (the cost metric).
On the selected cost metric detail page, scroll down to find the section titled "Configuration example" or similar that shows a JSON configuration block.
Click inside the JSON configuration example and locate the field named `"price_per_1k_tokens"`, then set its numeric value to `0.002`.
In the same JSON example, ensure there are fields named `"currency"` and `"unit"`, setting `"currency"` to the string `"USD"` and `"unit"` to the string `"tokens"`.
Click the "Copy JSON" or generic "Copy" button associated with this JSON configuration block to copy the updated metric configuration.

tasks.name: Configure a comparison evaluation with two candidate models and a baseline using the config builder
tasks.description: Using the interactive config builder in the docs, create a text generation evaluation that compares models "candidate_a" and "candidate_b" against baseline "baseline_v1", sets temperatures to 0.2, 0.7, and 0.0 respectively, limits max_samples to 50, enables only accuracy and latency metrics, and generates the config.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click the "Guides" option in the top navigation bar.
In the Guides sidebar, select the entry named "Config builder" or "Configuration builder".
On the config builder page, locate the "Task type" dropdown and select the option labeled "Text generation".
In the "Models" or "Variants" section, click the button to add or edit model entries and set one row's model id to "candidate_a" and another row's model id to "candidate_b".
In the same section, add or identify a baseline row and set its model id to "baseline_v1".
For the "candidate_a" row, set the temperature field to `0.2`; for the "candidate_b" row, set the temperature field to `0.7`; and for the baseline row, set the temperature field to `0.0`.
Scroll to the "Evaluation settings" or similar panel and enter `50` into the `max_samples` (or similarly named) input field.
In the metrics selection area, use the checkboxes or toggles to select only "accuracy" and "latency" metrics, deselecting any other metrics that may be pre-selected.
Click the "Generate config" button to produce the evaluation configuration JSON or code based on these settings.

tasks.name: Find the JavaScript SDK batch evaluation example and set concurrency to 10
tasks.description: Using the docs search, locate the JavaScript SDK page that shows the runBatchEvaluation helper, modify the example so the concurrency option is set to 10, and copy the updated JavaScript snippet.
steps: Navigate to the documentation homepage.
Click on the global search input in the header and type the term `runBatchEvaluation`.
Press the Enter key to execute the documentation search.
In the search results list, click the result that is labeled as part of the "JavaScript SDK" and references batch evaluations.
On the selected JavaScript SDK page, locate the language or SDK tabs and ensure the "JavaScript" tab is active.
Scroll to the example code block that calls the `runBatchEvaluation` function.
Within that code block, locate the configuration object or options argument passed to `runBatchEvaluation` and change the `concurrency` (or similarly named) field to the number `10`.
Click the code block's "Copy" icon or button to copy the modified JavaScript example with concurrency set to 10.

tasks.name: Locate a BLEU-based evaluation example with at least 1000 samples and copy its endpoint path
tasks.description: In the examples gallery, filter to show only evaluations using the BLEU metric and datasets with a minimum of 1000 samples, open the third matching example, and copy the REST API endpoint path shown on that page.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click the "Examples" item in the top navigation bar.
On the Examples gallery page, locate the metric filter dropdown and select the metric labeled "BLEU".
Find the dataset size filter control and set the minimum dataset size value to `1000`.
Click the "Apply filters" or equivalent button to update the gallery with the BLEU metric and minimum 1000 samples constraint.
Scroll through the filtered results and click on the third example card in the list of displayed examples.
On the example detail page, scroll down to find the section labeled "REST API" or "API endpoint".
Within that section, locate the displayed endpoint path that begins with `/v1/projects/` or `/v2/projects/` under an "Endpoint" label.
Click the small copy icon or button located next to this endpoint path to copy the endpoint string.

tasks.name: Switch docs to API version v2 and copy the dataset creation endpoint curl example
tasks.description: Change the documentation version from v1 to v2, open the API reference for the dataset creation endpoint, and copy the curl example that calls the v2 dataset creation path.
steps: Go to the documentation homepage.
At the top of the page, click the API version selector dropdown that currently shows "v1" or another version.
From the version dropdown, select the entry labeled "v2" to switch the docs to API version 2.
After the version switch, click the "API Reference" item in the top navigation bar.
In the API sidebar, open the "Datasets" section and click on the "Create dataset" endpoint entry.
On the v2 Create dataset endpoint page, scroll to the section that displays example requests.
In the language tabs for the examples, select the "curl" tab if it is not already active.
Click the "Copy" icon on the curl example that invokes the POST `/v2/datasets` (or `/v2/projects/{project_id}/datasets`) path to copy the v2 dataset creation request.

tasks.name: Run a sample evaluation in the playground with weighted accuracy (0.7) and latency (0.3)
tasks.description: Using the interactive playground in the docs, configure a text classification evaluation with accuracy weight 0.7 and latency weight 0.3, a sample size of 10, the evaluation name "weighted_test_01", and run the evaluation.
steps: From the documentation homepage, click the "Playground" link in the top navigation bar.
On the playground page, locate the "Task type" selector and choose the option labeled "Text classification".
In the prompt input or textarea, type a short instruction such as "Classify this review as positive or negative."

In the metrics configuration area, adjust the controls so that the accuracy metric has a weight of `0.7` and the latency metric has a weight of `0.3`, deselecting or setting weights to 0 for all other metrics.
Locate the input field labeled "Sample size" or similar and enter the value `10`.
Find the field labeled "Evaluation name" or "Run name" and type the string `weighted_test_01`.
If there is a model selector, keep the default model or select any available model entry without changing other advanced parameters.
Click the "Run evaluation" button to start the sample evaluation with the configured settings.

entities.name: DocPage
entities.description: Documentation page in the AI evaluation docs site.

entities.name: NavigationLink
entities.description: Top header and footer navigation links.

fields.name: targetPageId
fields.description: ID of the DocPage this link navigates to.

entities.name: SidebarItem
entities.description: Items shown in left sidebars for various sections (Getting Started, API, Concepts, Guides, etc.).

fields.name: parentPageId
fields.description: DocPage that owns this sidebar.

fields.name: targetPageId
fields.description: DocPage this sidebar item links to.

entities.name: ApiGroup
entities.description: Logical group of API endpoints (e.g., Evaluations, Datasets).

entities.name: ApiEndpoint
entities.description: REST API endpoint reference (e.g., Create Batch Evaluation, Create Dataset).

fields.name: groupId
fields.description: ApiGroup this endpoint belongs to.

fields.name: pathTemplate
fields.description: URI template including path params, e.g., /v1/projects/{project_id}/evaluations:batch.

fields.name: pathParams
fields.description: Array of path parameter definitions (e.g., project_id).

fields.name: queryParams
fields.description: Array of query parameter definitions (e.g., limit, cursor).

fields.name: docPageId
fields.description: DocPage describing this endpoint.

entities.name: ApiExample
entities.description: Per-language example requests/responses for an API endpoint (e.g., curl example).

fields.name: apiEndpointId
fields.description: ApiEndpoint this example belongs to.

fields.name: code
fields.description: Full example code snippet shown in docs.

entities.name: CodeSnippet
entities.description: Generic code block shown on a documentation page (quickstarts, SDK usage, JSON config examples, generated configs, etc.).

fields.name: docPageId
fields.description: DocPage where this snippet is displayed.

fields.name: code
fields.description: The current editable code content of the snippet.

fields.name: relatedEntityId
fields.description: ID of a related entity (e.g., metricId, sdkMethodId, evaluationConfigSnippetId).

entities.name: EvaluationConfigSnippet
entities.description: Structured representation of an evaluation configuration embedded in a code snippet (Python quickstart, builder output, etc.).

fields.name: codeSnippetId
fields.description: CodeSnippet that contains this config.

fields.name: metrics
fields.description: Array of metric IDs (e.g., ["accuracy", "f1"]).

fields.name: maxSamples
fields.description: max_samples or equivalent field.

fields.name: modelId
fields.description: Underlying model identifier used in this config (e.g., gpt-4.1-mini).

entities.name: Metric
entities.description: Evaluation metric (e.g., accuracy, F1, latency, BLEU, cost).

fields.name: id
fields.description: Metric identifier, e.g., accuracy, f1, latency, bleu, cost.

fields.name: isTokenBillingRelated
fields.description: True for the cost metric whose description mentions token-based billing.

fields.name: metricDetailPageId
fields.description: DocPage that shows this metric's detail/configuration.

entities.name: Project
entities.description: Logical project used in API paths (e.g., /projects/{project_id}).

fields.name: id
fields.description: Project identifier, e.g., proj_123.

entities.name: Dataset
entities.description: Dataset used for evaluations and created via the dataset creation endpoint.

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project that owns this dataset.

fields.name: size
fields.description: Number of samples in the dataset.

entities.name: EvaluationExample
entities.description: End-to-end evaluation example shown in the Examples gallery.

fields.name: metricIds
fields.description: Array of metrics used in this example (e.g., includes "bleu").

fields.name: datasetId
fields.description: Dataset used in this example.

fields.name: datasetSize
fields.description: Dataset size for filtering by minimum 1000 samples.

fields.name: exampleDetailPageId
fields.description: DocPage that shows the example's detail view.

fields.name: endpointPath
fields.description: REST API endpoint path displayed on the example detail page (e.g., /v1/projects/... or /v2/projects/...).

entities.name: ExamplesGalleryFilterState
entities.description: Current filter settings on the Examples gallery page.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Single instance identifier for filter state.

fields.name: selectedMetricId
fields.description: Metric ID selected in the metric filter dropdown (e.g., bleu).

fields.name: minDatasetSize
fields.description: Minimum dataset size filter (e.g., 1000).

entities.name: DocsVersion
entities.description: Available API/docs versions (e.g., v1, v2) selectable in the version selector.

fields.name: defaultApiBaseUrl
fields.description: Base URL associated with this docs version.

entities.name: SiteSettings
entities.description: Global settings for the docs UI, including currently selected API version.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Singleton ID, e.g., site.

entities.name: PlaygroundEvaluationRun
entities.description: Evaluation runs created from the interactive Playground.

fields.name: accuracyWeight
fields.description: Weight applied to accuracy metric (e.g., 0.7).

fields.name: latencyWeight
fields.description: Weight applied to latency metric (e.g., 0.3).

fields.name: metricIds
fields.description: Selected metrics IDs for this Playground run.

fields.name: sampleSize
fields.description: Number of samples (e.g., 10).

fields.name: evaluationName
fields.description: Name of the run (e.g., weighted_test_01).

fields.name: modelId
fields.description: Model selected in the Playground.

entities.name: ConfigBuilderState
entities.description: Current configuration in the interactive Config Builder guide.

fields.name: modelVariantIds
fields.description: Array of ModelVariant IDs (candidates and baseline).

fields.name: maxSamples
fields.description: Global max_samples value (e.g., 50).

fields.name: selectedMetricIds
fields.description: Metrics enabled in the builder (e.g., ["accuracy", "latency"]).

fields.name: generatedConfigSnippetId
fields.description: CodeSnippet ID of the last generated configuration.

entities.name: ModelVariant
entities.description: Model entry in the Config Builder (candidate or baseline) with its temperature and identifier.

fields.name: configBuilderStateId
fields.description: ConfigBuilderState this variant belongs to.

fields.name: temperature
fields.description: Sampling temperature (e.g., 0.2, 0.7, 0.0).

entities.name: SearchIndexEntry
entities.description: Indexed term mapping for global documentation search.

fields.name: docPageId
fields.description: DocPage that this term matches.

fields.name: snippet
fields.description: Short matching text around the search term.

entities.name: SearchResult
entities.description: Stored results for a given search query execution.

fields.name: entryIds
fields.description: Array of SearchIndexEntry IDs returned for this query.

entities.name: SdkMethod
entities.description: Documented SDK method, such as runBatchEvaluation in the JavaScript SDK.

fields.name: docPageId
fields.description: DocPage where this SDK method is documented.

fields.name: exampleSnippetId
fields.description: CodeSnippet ID of the main example for this method.

entities.name: BatchEvaluationRun
entities.description: Represents a batch evaluation run created via the batch evaluation endpoint.

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project this batch run is associated with.

fields.name: limit
fields.description: Pagination limit used in the request (e.g., 50).

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Pagination cursor used in the request (e.g., cursor_abc).
